about.full:
15+ years of academic experience in Computer Science, Geo-AI, and XR technologies

Contributed as a senior researcher to large-scale national projects totaling over $9.3M in funding, collaborating under Sejong University principal investigators.

Published 200+ peer-reviewed papers in top-tier journals and int'l confs

Secured 51+ patents (US and Korean) in spatial analysis and XR technologies. 

Exploring culturally-grounded Physical AI: currently developing frameworks and prototypes for intelligent robots and XR agents capable of using geospatial awareness and affective signals to interpret and convey cultural context.

Initiating cross-cultural Geo-AI collaborations: leveraging multicultural experience to design future AI/VR research that bridges Korean and Middle Eastern cultural contexts through spatial and immersive intelligence.

Curriculum Innovation (in progress): proposing and designing specialized future courses on AI for Art & Culture, Embodied Cultural Intelligence, and GeoAI for Heritage — integrating technical AI skills with artistic, cultural, and cross-regional applications. 

Contributed in launching multiple research centers, including the XR Metaverse Research Center

Supervised 40+ Master's and 6+ Ph.D. students

Established international collaborations with institutions worldwide

Pioneered integration of AI with GIS, XR, and Physical/Embodied systems.

Received multiple prestigious awards including Australian Endeavour Fellowship

Demonstrated success in industry-academia collaboration

Overview

During the faculty position, he has been teaching various classes related to Computer Science and Engineering specially Virtual Reality, Mixed Reality, Geo-AI, Geo-informatics. He supervised more than 40 Master and six PhD students directly and indirectly during my 15 years research in various universities. Long time experiences in performing research and teaching in academic areas, makes me capable to perform a considerable contribution to postgraduate research student supervision. 

Planning and designing new graduate-level courses such as AI for Art and Culture, Embodied Cultural Intelligence, Physical GeoAI, and Affective XR for Cultural Experiences, aligning with the emerging focus on AI-driven creative technologies.

Academic Teaching Positions

Associate Professor | Department of Computer Science and Engineering, Sejong University, South Korea (March 2017 - Present)

Teaching Areas

Delivered hands-on workshops and courses on Python and R programming for GIS, machine learning, and big data technologies.

Integrated tools like TensorFlow, Orange Data Mining, and Apache Spark into classroom learning.

Introduced hands-on sessions using LangChain for integrating NLP and LLM into real-world applications.

profile.name: Dr. Abolghasem Sadeghi
profile.title: Associate Professor
profile.summary:
Dr. Abolghasem Sadeghi-Niaraki is an Associate Professor in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at the XR (eXtended Reality) Metaverse Research Center, Sejong University, Republic of Korea, where he has been a faculty member since 2017. From 2009 to 2017, he served as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Geo-Informatics Engineering at INHA University. He was honored as one of the top 100 international distinguished researchers by the Australian Government, receiving the prestigious Endeavour Fellowship Award in 2012, during which he was a visiting scholar at the University of Melbourne. He was recently appointed as a Fellow at the Spatial Data Lab (SDL) within the Center for Geographic Analysis at Harvard University—one of the premier research institutions in the United States dedicated to spatial data analysis. In this role, he conducts collaborative research and development in spatiotemporal data analytics, visualization, and geo-AI-based platforms, contributing to innovative geographic data Environmental applications and natural hazards, especially floods and their impact on the topic of climate change in the USA. Invited as a non-salaried Visiting Professor at the University of Maryland, Baltimore County (UMBC), reflecting international collaboration between Korea and the U.S. in GeoAI and Culture Technology. Dr. Abolghasem is listed among the Top 2% Scientists in the Stanford-Elsevier dataset for 2024 for his outstanding contributions to Geo-AI and Geospatial Engineering. He is also a member of the American Association of Geographers (AAG), the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE), KCGS and ASIAGRAPHICS, contributing to global discussions on geographic analysis, spatial data, and Geo-AI technologies.

With a strong academic foundation including a Ph.D. in Geo-Informatics Engineering from INHA University and degrees from K. N. Toosi University of Technology (KNTU), Dr. Sadeghi-Niaraki has established himself as a prolific researcher advancing the fields of Geo-AI, GIS, HCI, IoT, Metaverse, and Physical AI (embodied intelligence) for cultural technology. His groundbreaking research in Geo-AI, Virtual Reality (VR), Mixed Reality (MR), and Extended Reality (XR) applications within GIS, supported by the Korean Ministry of Science and ICT, has pushed the boundaries of immersive technologies in spatial data systems.

Seasoned researcher and educator with extensive experience in Geo-AI, Artificial Intelligence, Natural Language Processing (NLP), and Machine Learning, combining geospatial analysis with AI frameworks. Skilled in leveraging cutting-edge tools like TensorFlow, Hugging Face, LangChain, and Replicate for developing advanced NLP and LLM applications. Published two books on Python programming for GIS applications. Proficient in big data technologies, including Apache Spark, PySpark, and Hadoop, with additional expertise in web development, XR technologies, and bioinformatics sensors. He is extending this expertise into emotion-aware XR and cultural intelligence, developing immersive systems that integrate affective computing with spatial/geospatial AI for art and heritage applications. 

His leadership in fostering international collaboration has resulted in significant initiatives, including:

Served as a Key Contributor in Establishing the Super-Realistic (Metaverse) XR Research Center

Contributed as a Core Team Member in Launching the Mobile Virtual Reality Research Center (MVR-RC):

Establishing international postgraduate programs in Culture Technology with KAIST

Securing Korea Knowledge Sharing Program (KSP) projects with Electronics and Telecommunications Research Institute

Consulting for major Korean companies including HANCOM Group and KSIC.net

Implementing innovative solutions like the ubiquitous GIS project at Gimpo Airport
